name: address
detail: Configure the Cisco TrustSec policy-server address.
syntax: address {domain-name name | ipv4 policy-server-address | ipv6 policy-server-address}

params.name: domain-name name
params.description: Specifies the domain name of the policy server.

params.name: ipv4 policy-server-address
params.description: Specifies the IP address of the policy server.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: Specifies the IPv6 address of the policy server.

usageSummary: Configure the policy server name to enter the policy-server configuration mode.

name: clear cts environment-data
detail: Clear Cisco TrustSec environment data.
syntax: clear cts environment-data This command has no arguments or keywords.

name: clear cts policy-server statistics
detail: Clear Cisco TrustSec policy-server statistics.
syntax: clear cts policy-server statistics {active | all}

params.name: active
params.description: Clears statistics of all active policy servers.

name: content-type json
detail: Enable the JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) as the content type.
syntax: content-type json This command has no arguments or keywords.
usageSummary: JSON is used as the content-type to download Security Group access control lists (SGACLs) and environment data from the Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE).

name: cts authorization list
detail: Specify a list of authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) servers to be used by the TrustSec seed device.
syntax: cts authorization list server_list
usageSummary: This command is only for the seed device.  Non-seed devices obtain the TrustSec AAA server list from their TrustSec authenticator peer as a component of their TrustSec environment data.  The following example displays an AAA configuration of a TrustSec seed device: Device# cts credentials id Device1 password Cisco123 Device# configure terminal Device(config)# aaa new-model Device(config)# aaa authentication dot1x default group radius Device(config)# aaa authorization network MLIST group radius Device(config)# cts authorization list MLIST Device(config)# aaa accounting dot1x default start-stop group radius Device(config)# radius-server host 10.

name: cts change-password
detail: Change the password between the local device and the authentication server.
syntax: cts change-password server ipv4_address udp_port {a-id hex_string | key radius_key }[source interface_list]

params.name: ipv4_address
params.description: IP address of the authentication server.

params.name: udp_port
params.description: UPD port of the authentication server.

params.name: a-id hex_string
params.description: Specifies the identification string of the ACS server.

params.name: key
params.description: Specifies the RADIUS key to be used for provisioning. source interface_list (Optional) Specifies the interface type and its identifying parameters as per the displayed list for source address in request packets.

usageSummary: The cts change-password command allows an administrator to change the password used between the local device and the Cisco Secure ACS authentication server, without having to reconfigure the authentication server.  The following example shows how to change the Cisco TrustSec password between a switch and a Cisco Secure ACS: Device# cts change-password server 192. 168.

name: cts credentials
detail: Use the cts credentials command in privileged EXEC mode to specify the TrustSec ID and password of the network device. Use the clear cts credentials command to delete the credentials.
syntax: cts credentials id cts_id password cts_pwd

params.name: password cts_pwd
params.description: Specifies the password for this device to use when authenticating with other Cisco TrustSec devices with EAP-FAST.

usageSummary: Important The cts credentialscommand must be configured only in privileged EXEC mode.  Do not use global configuration (config) mode to configure the cts credentials command.  The cts credentials command specifies the Cisco TrustSec device ID and password for this device to use when authenticating with other Cisco TrustSec devices with EAP-FAST.

name: cts environment-data enable
detail: Enable the download of environment data through REST application programming interfaces (APIs).
syntax: cts environment-data enable This command has no arguments or keywords.
usageSummary: The cts environment-data enable command cannot co-exist with the cts authorization list command.  The cts authorization list command enables the download of environment data through RADIUS.  If you try to configure RADIUS-based configuration by using the cts authorization list command, when the cts environment-data enable command is already configured, the following error message is displayed on the console: Error: 'cts policy-server or cts environment-data' related configs are enabled.

name: cts policy-server device-id
detail: Configure the policy-server device ID.
syntax: cts policy-server device-id device-ID

params.name: device-ID
params.description: Device ID of the Cisco TrustSec device.

usageSummary: The device ID must be the same one that was used to add the network access device (NAD) on Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE).  This ID is used to send environment data requests to Cisco ISE.

name: cts policy-server name
detail: Configure a Cisco TrustSec policy server and enter policy-server configuration mode.
syntax: cts policy-server name server-name
usageSummary: The policy server name will accept all characters.  Once the policy-server name is configured, the configuration mode changes to policy-server configuration.  You can configure other details of the policy-server in this mode.

name: cts policy-server order random
detail: Change the server-selection logic to random.
syntax: cts policy-server order random This command has no arguments or keywords.
usageSummary: When multiple HTTP policy servers are configured on a device, a single Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) instance may get overloaded if the device always selects the first configured server.  To avoid this situation, each device randomly selects a server.  A random number is generated by the device and based on this number a server is selected.

name: cts policy-server username
detail: Configure a policy-server username.
syntax: cts policy-server username username password {0 | 6 | 7 password} password

params.name: username
params.description: Username to access REST application programming interfaces (APIs).

params.name: password
params.description: Specifies the password to authenticate the user.

usageSummary: You must configure the username and password in Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) as the REST API access credentials, before configuring it on the device.  See the Cisco TrustSec HTTP Servers section of the "Cisco TrustSec Policies Configuration" chapter for more information.

name: cts refresh
detail: Refresh the TrustSec peer authorization policy of all or specific Cisco TrustSec peers, or to refresh the SGACL policies downloaded to the device by the authentication server.
syntax: cts refresh {peer [peer_id] | sgt [sgt_number | default | unknown]}

params.name: peer Peer-ID
params.description: (Optional) If a peer-id is specified, only policies related to the specified peer connection are refreshed.

params.name: sgt sgt_number
params.description: (Optional) Performs an immediate refresh of the SGACL policies from the authentication server. If an SGT number is specified, only policies related to that SGT are refreshed.

usageSummary: To refresh the Peer Authorization Policy on all TrustSec peers, enter cts policy refresh without specifying a peer ID.  The peer authorization policy is initially downloaded from the Cisco ACS at the end of the EAP-FAST NDAC authentication success.  The Cisco ACS is configured to refresh the peer authorization policy, but the cts policy refresh command can force immediate refresh of the policy before the Cisco ACS timer expires.

name: cts rekey
detail: Regenerate the Pairwise Master Key used by the Security Association Protocol (SAP).
syntax: cts rekey interface type slot/port

params.name: interface
params.description: type slot/port Specifies the Cisco TrustSec interface on which to regenerate the SAP key.

usageSummary: SAP Pair-wise Master Key key (PMK) refresh ordinarily occurs automatically, triggered by combinations of network events and non-configurable internal timers related to dot1X authentication.  The ability to manually refresh encryption keys is often part of network administration security requirements.  To manually force a PMK refresh, use the cts rekey command.

name: cts role-based enforcement
detail: Enable role-based access control globally and on specific Layer 3 interfaces using Cisco TrustSec.
syntax: cts role-based enforcement
usageSummary: The cts role-based enforcement command in global configuration mode enables role-based access control globally.  Once role-based access control is enabled globally, it is automatically enabled on every Layer 3 interface on the device.  To disable role-based access control on specific Layer 3 interfaces, use the no form of the command in interface configuration mode.

name: cts role-based l2-vrf
detail: Select a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance for Layer 2 VLANs.
syntax: cts role-based l2-vrf vrf-name vlan-list {all vlan-ID} [,] [-]
noForm: no cts role-based l2-vrf vrf-name vlan-list {all vlan-ID} [,] [-]

params.name: vlan-list
params.description: Specifies the list of VLANs to be assigned to a VRF instance.

params.name: vlan-ID
params.description: VLAN ID. Valid values are from 1 to 4094.
(Optional) Specifies another VLAN separated by a comma.
(Optional) Specifies a range of VLANs separated by a hyphen.

usageSummary: The vlan-list argument can be a single VLAN ID, a list of comma-separated VLAN IDs, or hyphen-separated VLAN ID ranges.  The all keyword is equivalent to the full range of VLANs supported by the network device.  The all keyword is not preserved in the nonvolatile generation (NVGEN) process.

name: cts role-based monitor
detail: Enable role-based (security-group) access list monitoring.
syntax: cts role-based monitor {all | permissions {default [ipv4 | ipv6] | from {sgt | unknown} to {sgt | unknown} [ipv4 | ipv6]}} | unknown} [ipv4 | ipv6]}}
noForm: no cts role-based monitor {all | permissions {default [ipv4 | ipv6] | from {sgt | unknown} to {sgt

params.name: all
params.description: Monitors permissions for all source tags to all destination tags.

params.name: permissions
params.description: Monitors permissions from a source tags to a destination tags.

params.name: from
params.description: Specifies the source group tag for filtered traffic.

params.name: sgt
params.description: Security Group Tag (SGT). Valid values are from 2 to 65519.

params.name: unknown
params.description: Specifies an unknown source or destination group tag (DST).

usageSummary: Use the cts role-based monitor all command to enable the global monitor mode.  If the cts role-based monitor all command is configured, the output of the show cts role-based permissions command displays monitor mode for all configured policies as true.  The following examples shows how to configure SGACL monitor from a source tag to a destination tag: Device(config)# cts role-based monitor permissions from 10 to 11 Related Commands Command Description show cts role-based permissions Displays the SGACL permission list.

name: cts role-based permissions
detail: Enable permissions from a source group to a destination group.
syntax: cts role-based permissions {default | from {sgt | unknown}to {sgt | unknown}} {rbacl-name | ipv4 | ipv6} ipv4 | ipv6}
noForm: no cts role-based permissions {default | from {sgt | unknown}to {sgt | unknown}} {rbacl-name |

params.name: default
params.description: Specifies the default permissions list. Every cell (an SGT pair) for which, security group access control list (SGACL) permission is not configured statically or dynamically falls under the default category.

params.name: from
params.description: Specifies the source group tag of the filtered traffic.

params.name: sgt
params.description: Security Group Tag (SGT). Valid values are from 2 to 65519.

params.name: unknown
params.description: Specifies an unknown source or destination group tag. rbacl-name Role-based access control list (RBACL) or SGACL name. Up to 16 SGACLs can be specified in the configuration.

usageSummary: Use the cts role-based permissions command to define, replace, or delete the list of SGACLs for a given source group tag (SGT), destination group tag (DGT) pair.  This policy is in effect as long as there is no dynamic policy for the same DGT or SGT.  The cts role-based permissions default command defines, replaces, or deletes the list of SGACLs of the default policy as long as there is no dynamic policy for the same DGT.

name: cts role-based sgt-caching
detail: Enable Security Group Tag (SGT) caching globally.
syntax: cts role-based sgt-caching [vlan-list {vlan-id | all}]

params.name: vlan-list vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies VLAN IDs. Individual VLAN IDs are separated by commas, and a range of IDs specified with a hyphen. Valid values are from 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: To enable SGT caching on a VLAN, both cts role-based sgt-caching and cts role-based sgt-caching vlan-list commands must be configured.

name: cts role-based sgt-map
detail: Manually map a source IP address to a Security Group Tag (SGT) on either a host or a VRF.
syntax: cts role-based sgt-map {ipv4_netaddress|ipv6_netaddress|ipv4_netaddress/prefix|ipv6_netaddress/prefix} sgt sgt-number cts role-based sgt-map host {ipv4_hostaddress|ipv6_hostaddress} sgt sgt-number cts role-based sgt-map vlan-list [vlan_ids|all] sgt sgt-number cts role-based sgt-map vrf instance_name {ipv4_netaddress|ipv6_netaddress|ipv4_netaddress/prefix|ipv6_netaddress/prefix|host {ipv4_hostaddress|ipv6_hostaddress}} sgt sgt-number

params.name: ipv4_netaddress |
params.description: Specifies the network to be associated with an SGT. Enter IPv4 address

params.name: ipv6_netaddress
params.description: in dot decimal notation; IPv6 in colon hexadecimal notation.

params.name: ipv4_netaddress/prefix |
params.description: Maps the SGT to all hosts of the specified subnet address (IPv4 or

params.name: ipv6_netaddress/prefix
params.description: IPv6). IPv4 is specified in dot decimal CIDR notation, IPv6 in colon hexadecimal notation

params.name: host {ipv4_hostaddress |
params.description: Binds the specified host IP address with the SGT. Enter the IPv4

params.name: ipv6_hostaddress}
params.description: address in dot decimal notation; IPv6 in colon hexadecimal notation.

params.name: vlan-list {vlan_ids | all}
params.description: Specifies VLAN IDs. • (Optional) vlan_ids: Individual VLAN IDs are separated by commas, a range of IDs specified with a hyphen. • (Optional) all: Specifies all VLAN IDs.

params.name: vrf instance_name
params.description: Specifies a VRF instance, previously created on the device.

params.name: sgt sgt-number
params.description: Specifies the SGT number from 0 to 65,535.

usageSummary: If you do not have a Cisco Identity Services Engine, Cisco Secure ACS, dynamic Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inspection, Dynamic Host Control Protocol (DHCP) snooping, or Host Tracking available on your Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18. x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 122 Cisco TrustSec cts role-based sgt-map device to automatically map SGTs to source IP addresses, you can manually map an SGT to the following with the cts role-based sgt-map command: • A single host IPv4 or IPv6 address • All hosts of an IPv4 or IPv6 network or subnetwork • VRFs • Single or multiple VLANs The cts role-based sgt-map command binds the specified SGT with packets that fall within the specified network address.

name: cts sxp connection peer
detail: Enter the Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) peer IP address, to specify if a password is used for the peer connection, to specify the global hold-time period for a listener or speaker device, and to specify if the connection is bidirectional.
syntax: cts sxp connection peer ipv4-address {source | password} {default | none} mode {local | peer} [[[listener | speaker] [hold-time minimum-time maximum-time | vrf vrf-name]] | both [vrf vrf-name]] cts sxp connection peer ipv4-address {source | password} {default | none} mode {local | peer} [[[listener | speaker] [hold-time minimum-time maximum-time | vrf vrf-name]] | both [vrf vrf-name]]

params.name: password
params.description: Specifies that an SXP password is used for the peer connection.

params.name: default
params.description: Specifies that the default SXP password is used.

params.name: mode
params.description: Specifies either the local or peer SXP connection mode.

params.name: local
params.description: Specifies that the SXP connection mode refers to the local device.

params.name: peer
params.description: Specifies that the SXP connection mode refers to the peer device.

params.name: listener
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the device is the listener in the connection.

params.name: speaker
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the device is the speaker in the connection.

params.name: hold-time minimum-time
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the hold-time period, in seconds, for the device. The range

params.name: maximum-time
params.description: for minimum and maximum time is from 0 to 65535. A maximum-time value is required only when you use the following keywords: peer speaker and local listener. In other instances, only a minimum-time value is required. Note If both minimum and maximum times are required, the maximum-time value must be greater than or equal to the minimum-time value.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance name to the peer.

params.name: both
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the device is both the speaker and the listener in the bidirectional SXP connection. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 124 Cisco TrustSec cts sxp connection peer

usageSummary: When a CTS-SXP connection to a peer is configured with the cts sxp connection peer command, only the connection mode can be changed.  The vrf keyword is optional.  If a VRF name is not provided or a VRF name is provided with the default keyword, then the connection is set up in the default routing or forwarding domain.

name: cts sxp default password
detail: Specify the Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) default password.
syntax: cts sxp default password {0 unencrypted-pwd | 6 encrypted-key | 7 encrypted-keycleartext-pwd}
noForm: no cts sxp default password {0 unencrypted-pwd | 6 encrypted-key | 7 encrypted-keycleartext-pwd}

params.name: 6 encrypted-key
params.description: Specifies that a 6 encryption type password is used as the CTS-SXP default password. The maximum password length is 32 characters.

params.name: 7 encrypted-key
params.description: Specifies that a 7 encryption type password is used as the CTS-SXP default password. The maximum password length is 32 characters.

params.name: cleartext-pwd
params.description: Specifies a cleartext CTS-SXP default password. The maximum password length is 32 characters.

usageSummary: The cts sxp default password command sets the CTS-SXP default password to be optionally used for all CTS-SXP connections configured on the device.  The CTS-SXP password can be cleartext, or encrypted with the 0, 7, 6 encryption type keywords.  If the encryption type is 0, then an unencrypted cleartext password follows.

name: cts sxp default source-ip
detail: Configure the Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) source IPv4 address.
syntax: cts sxp default source-ip ipv4-address
noForm: no cts sxp default source-ip ipv4-address
usageSummary: The cts sxp default source-ip command sets the default source IP address that CTS-SXP uses for all new TCP connections where a source IP address is not specified.  Preexisting TCP connections are not affected when this command is entered.

name: cts sxp export-import-group
detail: Create an SXP export or import VRF group.
syntax: cts sxp export-import-group { listener | speaker } { vrf-group-name | global }
noForm: no cts sxp export-import-group { listener | speaker } { vrf-group-name | global }

params.name: listener
params.description: Creates an SXP listener import group.

params.name: speaker
params.description: Creates an SXP speaker export group. vrf-group-name Name of the export or import VRF group name.

params.name: global
params.description: Configures either an SXP listener global import-group or SXP speaker global export-group.

usageSummary: Export and import list configurations cannot be removed if it is associated with any SXP group.  Modifying a peer list under an SXP group is not supported when the peer connection configuration is present.

name: cts sxp export-list
detail: Create an SXP export list of VRF bindings to be exported to the listener.
syntax: cts sxp export-list export-list-name
usageSummary: Export list configurations cannot be removed if it is associated with any SXP group.  Modifying a peer list under an SXP group is not supported when the peer connection configuration is present.

name: cts sxp filter-enable
detail: Enable filtering after creating filter lists and filter groups.
syntax: cts sxp filter-enable
usageSummary: This command can be used at any time to enable or disable filtering.  Configured filter lists and filter groups can be used to implement filtering only after filtering is enabled.  The filter action will only filter bindings that are exchanged after filtering is enabled; there won’t be any effect on the bindings that were exchanged before filtering was enabled.

name: cts sxp filter-group
detail: Create a filter group for grouping a set of peers and applying a filter list to them.
syntax: cts sxp filter-group {listener | speaker} {filter-group-name | global filter-list-name}
noForm: no cts sxp filter-group {listener | speaker} {filter-group-name | global filter-list-name}

params.name: listener
params.description: Creates a filter group for a set of listeners.

params.name: speaker
params.description: Creates a filter group for a set of speakers.

params.name: global
params.description: Groups all speakers or listeners on the device.

usageSummary: Issuing this command, places the device in the filter group configuration mode.  From this mode, you can specify the devices to be grouped and apply a filter list to the filter group.  The command format to add devices or peers to the group is a follows: peer ipv4 peer-IP In a single command, you can add one peer.

name: cts sxp filter-list
detail: Create a SXP filter list to hold a set of filter rules for filtering IP-SGT bindings.
syntax: cts sxp filter-list filter-list-name
usageSummary: Issuing this command, places the device in the filter list configuration mode.  From this mode, you can specify rules for the filter lists.  A filter rule can be based on SGT or IP Prefixes or a combination of both SGT and IP Prefixes.

name: cts sxp import-list
detail: Create an SXP import list to hold VRFs where received SXP bindings are added.
syntax: cts sxp import-list import-list-name
usageSummary: Import list configurations cannot be removed if it is associated with any SXP group.  Modifying a peer list under an SXP group is not supported when the peer connection configuration is present.

name: cts sxp log binding-changes
detail: Enable logging for IP-to-Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) binding changes.
syntax: cts sxp log binding-changes
usageSummary: The cts sxp log binding-changes command enables logging for IP-to-SGT binding changes.  SXP syslogs (sev 5 syslogs) are generated whenever IP address-to-SGT binding occurs (add, delete, change).  These changes are learned and propagated on the SXP connection.

name: cts sxp reconciliation period
detail: Change the Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) reconciliation period.
syntax: cts sxp reconciliation period seconds
noForm: no cts sxp reconciliation period seconds

params.name: seconds
params.description: CTS-SXP reconciliation timer in seconds. The range is from 0 to 64000. The default is 120.

usageSummary: After a peer terminates a CTS-SXP connection, an internal delete hold-down timer starts.  If the peer reconnects before the delete hold-down timer expires, then the CTS-SXP reconciliation timer starts.  While the CTS-SXP reconciliation period timer is active, the CTS-SXP software retains the SGT mapping entries learned from the previous connection and removes invalid entries.

name: cts sxp retry period
detail: Change the Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) retry period timer.
syntax: cts sxpretry period seconds

params.name: seconds
params.description: CTS-SXP retry timer in seconds. The range is from 0 to 64000. The default is 120.

usageSummary: The retry timer is triggered if there is at least one CTS-SXP connection that is not up.  A new CTS-SXP connection is attempted when this timer expires.  A zero value results in no retry being attempted.

name: debug cts environment-data
detail: Enable the debugging of Cisco TrustSec environment data operations.
syntax: debug cts environment-data [aaa | all | default-epg | default-sg | events | platform | sg-epg]
noForm: no debug cts environment-data [aaa | all | default-epg | default-sg | events | platform | sg-epg]

params.name: aaa
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) messages.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of all environment-data messages.

params.name: default-epg
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of default end-point group (EPG) messages.

params.name: default-sg
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of default server group messages.

params.name: events
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of environment data events.

params.name: platform
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of Security Group Tag (SGT)-EPG platform messages.

params.name: sg-epg
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the debugging of SP-EPG mapping.

name: debug cts policy-server
detail: Enable Cisco TrustSec policy-server debugging.
syntax: debug cts policy-server {all | {http | json} {all | error | events}}

name: port
detail: Configure the policy server port.
syntax: port port-number

params.name: port-number
params.description: Policy server port number. Valid values are from 1025 to 65535.

usageSummary: Only 9063 is supported as the External RESTful Services (ERS) port.

name: propagate sgt
detail: Enable Security Group Tag (SGT) propagation at Layer 2 on Cisco TrustSec Security (CTS) interfaces.
syntax: propagate sgt
usageSummary: SGT processing propagation allows a CTS-capable interface to accept and transmit a CTS Meta Data (CMD) based L2 SGT tag.  The no propagate sgt command can be used to disable SGT propagation on an interface in situations where a peer device is not capable of receiving an SGT, and as a result, the SGT tag cannot be put in the L2 header.

name: retransmit
detail: Configure the maximum number of retries from the server.
syntax: retransmit number-of-retries

params.name: number-of-retries
params.description: Maximum number of retries. Valid values are from 0 to 5.

name: sap mode-list
detail: Select the Security Association Protocol (SAP) authentication and encryption modes (prioritized from highest to lowest) used to negotiate link encryption between two interfaces.
syntax: sap pmk mode-list {gcm-encrypt | gmac | no-encap | null} [gcm-encrypt | gmac | no-encap | null] | null]
noForm: no sap pmk mode-list {gcm-encrypt | gmac | no-encap | null} [gcm-encrypt | gmac | no-encap

params.name: pmk hex_value
params.description: Specifies the Hex-data PMK (without leading 0x; enter even number of hex characters, or else the last character is prefixed with 0.).

params.name: mode-list
params.description: Specifies the list of advertised modes (prioritized from highest to lowest).

params.name: gmac
params.description: Specifies GMAC authentication only, no encryption.

params.name: null
params.description: Specifies encapsulation present, no authentication, no encryption.

usageSummary: Use the sap pmk mode-list command to specify the authentication and encryption method.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: show cts credentials
detail: Display the Cisco TrustSec (CTS) device ID.
syntax: show cts credentials

params.name: This
params.description: command has no commands or keywords.

name: show cts environment-data
detail: Display Cisco TrustSec environment data information.
syntax: show cts environment-data This command has no arguments and keywords.

name: show cts interface
detail: Display Cisco TrustSec (CTS) configuration statistics for an interface(s).
syntax: show cts interface [GigabitEthernet port | Vlan number | brief | summary]

params.name: port
params.description: (Optional) Gigabit Ethernet interface number. A verbose status output for this interface is returned.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays abbreviated status for all CTS interfaces. summary (Optional) Displays a tabular summary of all CTS interfaces with 4 or 5 key status fields for each interface.

usageSummary: Use the show cts interface command without keywords to display verbose status for all CTS interfaces.

name: show cts policy-server
detail: Display Cisco TrustSec policy-server information.
syntax: show cts policy-server {details | statistics } {active | all name}

params.name: active
params.description: Displays information about active policy servers.

params.name: all
params.description: Displays statistics information about all servers.

name: show cts role-based counters
detail: Display Security Group access control list (ACL) enforcement statistics.
syntax: show cts role-based counters [default [ipv4 | ipv6]] [ {from {sgt-number | unknown}[ipv4 | ipv6 | to | {sgt-number | unknown} | [ipv4 | ipv6]]} ][to {sgt-number | unknown} [ipv4 | ipv6]] [ipv4 | ipv6]

params.name: default
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the default policy counters.

params.name: from
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the source security group.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about security groups on IPv4 networks.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about security groups on IPv6 networks.

params.name: to
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the destination security group.

params.name: sgt-number
params.description: (Optional) Security Group Tag number. Valid values are from 0 to 65533.

params.name: unknown
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about all source groups.

usageSummary: Use the clear cts role-based counterscommand to reset all or a range of statistics.  Specify the source SGT with the from keyword and the destination SGT with the to keyword.  All statistics are displayed when both the from and to keywords are omitted.

name: show cts role-based permissions
detail: Display the role-based (security group) access control permission list.
syntax: show cts role-based permissions [default [details | ipv4 [details] | ipv6 [details]] | from { {sgt | unknown }[ipv4 | ipv6 | to { {sgt | unknown}[details | ipv4 [details] | ipv6 [details]]}}] | ipv4 | ipv6 | platform | to {sgt | unknown}[ipv4 | ipv6]]

params.name: default
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the default permission list.

params.name: details
params.description: (Optional) Displays attached access control list (ACL) details.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the IPv4 protocol.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the IPv6 protocol.

params.name: from
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the source group.

params.name: sgt
params.description: (Optional) Security Group Tag. Valid values are from 2 to 65519.

params.name: to
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the destination group. unknown (Optional) Displays information about unknown source and destination groups. platform (Optional) Displays information about the platform.

usageSummary: This command displays the content of the SGACL permission matrix.  You can specify the source security group tag (SGT) by using the from keyword and the destination SGT by using the to keyword.  When both these keywords are specified RBACLs of a single cell are displayed.

name: show cts server-list
detail: Display the list of HTTP and RADIUS servers available to Cisco TrustSec seed and nonseed devices.
syntax: show cts server-list
usageSummary: This command is useful for gathering Cisco TrustSec RADIUS server address and status information.  In Cisco IOS XE Gibraltar 17. 1.

name: show cts sxp
detail: Display Cisco TrustSec Security Group Tag (SGT) Exchange Protocol (CTS-SXP) connection or source IP-to-SGT mapping information.
syntax: show cts sxp {connections [brief | vrf instance-name] | filter-group [detailed | global | listener | speaker ] | filter-list filter-list-name | sgt-map [brief | vrf instance-name]} [brief | vrf instance-name]

params.name: connections
params.description: Displays Cisco TrustSec SXP connections information.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays an abbreviation of the SXP information.

params.name: vrf instance-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays the SXP information for the specified Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF) instance name. filter-group {detailed | global | (Optional) Displays filter group information. listener | speaker }

params.name: sgt-map
params.description: (Optional) Displays the IP-to-SGT mappings received through SXP.

name: show platform hardware fed switch active sgacl resource usage
detail: Display Security Group access control list (SGACL) resource information for Application Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC).
syntax: show platform hardware fed switch active sgacl resource usage

name: show platform software classification switch active F0 class-group-manager class-group client acl all
syntax: show platform software classification switch active F0 class-group-manager class-group client acl all To display ACL class group ID, which is used to view Ternary Content Addressable Memory(TCAM) entry, use the show platform software classification switch active F0 class-group-manager class-group client acl all command in privileged EXEC mode. show platform software classification switch active F0 class-group-manager class-group client acl all

params.name: all
params.description: Displays the ACL class group ID for all class groups.

name: show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 port
syntax: Name The name of the interface. ID The interface ID. CTS The status of CTS. Enable Trusted The trusted status of the interface. Propagate The propagation status of the interface. SGT value The value of SGT. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 174 Cisco TrustSec show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 To display Security Group Tag (SGT) binding table, use the show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 command in privileged EXEC mode. show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0

name: show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 permissions
syntax: show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 permissions To display Security group access control lists (SGACLs) permissions, use the show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 permissions command in privileged EXEC mode. show platform software cts forwarding-manager switch active F0 permissions

params.name: F0
params.description: Selects embedded service processor slot 0. permissions Displays SGACL permissions.

name: show platform software fed switch active acl counters hardware | inc SGACL
syntax: show platform software fed switch active acl counters hardware | inc SGACL To display counters from the forwarding engine driver, use the show platform software fed switch active acl counters hardware | inc SGACL command in privileged EXEC mode. show platform software fed switch active acl counters hardware | inc SGACL

params.name: include
params.description: Includes lines that match the specified string.

name: show platform software fed switch active acl usage
detail: Display Security Group access control lists (SGACLs) usage.
syntax: show platform software fed switch active acl usage

name: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings
detail: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings

params.name: ifm
params.description: Displays interface manager information. mappings Displays interface to hardware mapping information.

name: show platform software fed switch active ip route
detail: Display IP route information.
syntax: show platform software fed switch active ip route

params.name: ip
params.description: Accepts IP commands. route Displays IPv4 Forwarding Information Base (FIB) details.

name: show platform software fed switch active sgacl detail
detail: Display global enforcement status along with policy and count information.
syntax: show platform software fed switch active sgacl detail

name: show platform software fed switch active sgacl port
detail: Display Layer 2 interface configuration settings for all interfaces.
syntax: show platform software fed switch active sgacl port

params.name: sgacl
params.description: Displays Security Group access control lists (SGACLs) hardware information.

params.name: Command History
params.description: Release Modification Cisco IOS XE Everest 16.5.1a This command was introduced. Example The following is a sample output from the show platform software fed switch active sgacl port command: Device# show platform software fed switch active sgacl port

params.name: Gi3/0/34
params.description: Disabled 0 No No No No Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 185 Cisco TrustSec show platform software fed switch active sgacl port

params.name: Ap3/0/1
params.description: Disabled 0 No No No No Output fields are self-explanatory. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 186 Cisco TrustSec show platform software fed switch active sgacl vlan

name: show platform software fed switch active sgacl vlan
detail: Display global enforcement status on VLANs.
syntax: show platform software fed switch active sgacl vlan

name: show platform software status control-processor brief
detail: Display brief information about CPU and memory.
syntax: show platform software status control-processor brief

name: show monitor capture <name> buffer
detail: Display the contents of a monitor capture buffer or a capture point.
syntax: show monitor capture name buffer

params.name: buffer
params.description: Displays the contents of the specified capture buffer.

params.name: name
params.description: Represents the name of the capture buffer.

name: timeout
detail: Configure the response timeout in seconds.
syntax: timeout seconds

params.name: seconds
params.description: Timeout in seconds. Valid values are from 1 to 60.

name: tls server-trustpoint
detail: Configures the Transport Layer Security (TLS) trustpoint, use the tls server-trustpoint command in policy-server configuration mode. To remove the TLS trustpoint, use the no form of this command.
syntax: tls server-trustpoint name
usageSummary: TLS is used by a network device to connect to the Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE).  The device uses a make or break approach to the TLS connection establishment, and there is no persistent TLS connection between the device and Cisco ISE.  After the TLS connection is established, the device can use this connection to submit multiple REST API calls to specific uniform resource locators (URLs).
